name: Syamantaka
description: Syamantaka is a legendary jewel in Hindu mythology, renowned for its extraordinary power to produce vast quantities of gold. Its origin traces back to Surya, the Sun God, who gifted it to his devotee, Satrajit, a Yadava nobleman. The jewel possessed the unique ability to generate eight measures of gold every day, ensuring prosperity and abundance for its owner. However, its immense power also brought misfortune. Satrajit's brother, Prasena, wearing the jewel, was killed by a lion in the forest. Jambavan, the king of bears, then killed the lion and took the jewel. The disappearance of Prasena and the Syamantaka jewel led to accusations against Krishna, who was suspected of foul play. To clear his name, Krishna embarked on a quest, eventually discovering Jambavan and engaging him in a fierce battle that lasted for twenty-eight days. Ultimately, Jambavan, recognizing Krishna as an avatar of Vishnu, surrendered, offering the jewel and his daughter Jambavati in marriage to Krishna. Krishna returned to Dwarka with the jewel and Jambavati, restoring Satrajit's honor and proving his own innocence. He later returned the Syamantaka jewel to Satrajit, understanding its potential for causing further conflict and misunderstanding. Satrajit, overwhelmed with guilt and gratitude, offered his daughter Satyabhama in marriage to Krishna. The Syamantaka jewel ultimately led to Satrajit's death at the hands of Shatadhanva, further highlighting the dangers associated with its immense power and the complexities of dharma.
subtitle: The Jewel of Boundless Gold
origin: Gift from Surya, the Sun God

keyMyths.title: The Gift of Surya
keyMyths.description: Surya gifts the Syamantaka jewel to Satrajit, granting him immense wealth and prosperity.

keyMyths.title: Krishna's Quest
keyMyths.description: Krishna embarks on a quest to recover the Syamantaka jewel and clear his name after being falsely accused of stealing it.

sources.author: K.M. Ganguli
